name: [BUG-SF-005] TC-SF-011 — POST /api/v1/fetch/status: Must return 405 Method Not Allowed (returns 500)
description:
**[KNOWN BUG — BUG-SF-005]**

**What this test does:**
Sends a POST request to /api/v1/fetch/status (a GET-only endpoint) and verifies the server returns 405 Method Not Allowed.

**Expected:** HTTP 405 Method Not Allowed
**Actual:** HTTP 500 Internal Server Error

**Root Cause:** The service lacks a global handler for unsupported HTTP methods. Instead of returning 405, Spring throws an unhandled MethodNotAllowedException which propagates to the default error handler and is returned as a generic 500.

**Impact:** API clients and automated scanners receive a misleading 500 error. The error response body does include a correlationId, which could aid debugging, but the wrong status code means automated monitoring treats this as a server crash rather than a normal invalid-method response.

Root Cause: The service lacks a global handler for unsupported HTTP methods. Instead of returning 405, Spring throws an unhandled MethodNotAllowedException which propagates to the default error handler and is returned as a generic 500.

Impact: API clients and automated scanners receive a misleading 500 error. The error response body does include a correlationId, which could aid debugging, but the wrong status code means automated monitoring treats this as a server crash rather than a normal invalid-method response.
